stories.headline: Harbaugh Says Nabers AND Skattebo Ride Into Camp, Saddle Up Twice
stories.read: Big smoke signal outta Giants country, pardner: coach John Harbaugh says Malik Nabers will be workin' his way into practice one way or another when camp opens in two weeks, and young Cam Skattebo is even further down the healin' trail. Nabers won't be full-go on day one, but the docs figure he's cleared before the preseason dust settles. Here's the play: that torn-up knee has the saloon pricin' a fella who commands a monster cut of the throws like damaged goods, and Skattebo's comin' even cheaper. Two discounted horses in the same corral, and the herd ain't caught wind yet.

stories.headline: Indy Wants to Lighten Jonathan Taylor's Load, Read That Twice Before You Draft
stories.read: The word from the Colts' ranch is that Jonathan Taylor's ready to haul another massive share of the freight in 2026, but the bosses are fixin' to ease up on the reins so their prize horse don't wear down. Now don't panic and sell the ranch, JT's still the bell cow, but every carry they shave off him lands in somebody else's saddlebag. That makes the spare horse behind him one of the cheapest insurance policies on the whole trail, and it puts a hair of doubt into payin' top dollar for Taylor at the saloon. Volume pays the rent, friend, and Indy just told you they're trimmin' it.

stories.headline: Five Outfits Come Sniffin' Around Stefon Diggs, the Old Gunslinger's Got Suitors
stories.read: Well now, the wire says five-plus teams have come knockin' on Stefon Diggs' door before the camps open, and the 32-year-old just posted a thousand-yard season to prove there's still gold in that claim. The man himself is tellin' anyone who'll listen that no second gun in the league shoots better than him. Here's the read: Diggs is a ghost at the saloon right now, nobody's draftin' a man without a ranch. The minute he signs somewhere with throws to spare, he's free land, so keep your powder dry and your waiver finger ready.
